oai_dc:dc.dc:title: HARI Philosophy on Nutrition part 1
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Allen, Melanie
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 51 Number 1 (January/February 2024).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.by Melanie Allen Avian Product Specialist Rolf C. Hagen (USA) Corp. Whether you manage a flock of many or a flock of one, appropriate nutrition is key to maintaining a healthy parrot. Without a doubt our choices in providing a healthy staple diet for our feathered friends have come a long way over the years, yet avian practitioners still see challenges with parrot species that involve poor diet. Most members of the avian community know how important good nutrition is and while we ’d like to think that our flock benefits from the healthy, scrumptiousfood we offer, we really need to take a review on the sure signs of benefit. Sometimes it ’s a matter of looking at your bird and knowing what to look for. Here's a few tips on how to tell if said bird is indeed benefiting.While HARI has been researching psittacine nutrition since 1985, the importance of appropriate nutrition plays a huge role in successful companion bird ownership and especially with breeding facilities.Let’s look at the two big culprits with obvious signs: Obesity and Lack of Vitamin A assimilation How to tell if a bird is overweight? Why is this important? And what is the risk? Overweight parrots often have underlying health risks that include fatty liver syndrome or hepatic lipidosis as well as arteriosclerosis and are candidates for heart and vascular challenges.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 51 No. 1 (2024): Watchbird January/February 2024; 4-5
